返回本批次

白领人工智能启示录的叙述只是又一派胡言

White-collar AI apocalypse narrative is just another bullshit

martynasm.com 作者 mmiliauskas 分数 59 评论 99 2026/03/23 16:16
打开 HN 原帖 打开原始文章

中文摘要

  1. 这是批判性思维的练习。
  2. 达里奥表示，“两年前人工智能还处于聪明的高中生的水平，现在它处于聪明的大学生的水平”1。客户支持市场现在一定已经被消灭了吗？但它自 2025 年中期以来正在反弹，现在略低于新冠疫情前的水平（又称正常水平）2？
  3. Indeed 美国客户服务职位发布
  4. 有两年的时间（对于 100 倍工程师来说是一千年），在此期间，公司可以将大量补贴的 OpenAI 模型与 RAG 连接起来，然后解雇所有人。相反，半年前他们就开始重新招聘人员？
  5. 这里唯一合理的结论是，你需要一个博士级别的人工智能来取代客户支持代理。

Original Summary

  1. Here is an exercise in critical thinking.
  2. According to Dario, “two years ago AI was at the level of a smart high school student, now it’s at the level of a smart college student“1. Customer Support market must be obliterated by now? Except it is bouncing back since the middle of 2025 and is now just below the pre-Covid levels (aka normal levels)2?
  3. Customer Service Job Postings on Indeed in the United States
  4. There were two years (which is a millennia for a 100x engineer) during which companies could have connected a heavily subsidized OpenAI model to a RAG and just fired everyone. Instead half a year ago they started hiring people back?
  5. The only rational conclusion here is that you need a PhD level AI to replace a customer support agent.

rspoerri 2026/03/23 17:44
人们往往忘记技术发展的速度有多快。即使你经历过它，你也往往会忘记世界最近看起来多么不同。

- 2012年之前没有智能手机

- 2001年之前没有维基百科

- 1995 年之前，只有不到 10% 的富裕国家家庭用户拥有互联网

- 2023 年之前，家庭用户无法使用人工智能。

硬件速度在 10 年内提高了 100 倍，在 20 年内提高了约 10,000 倍。由于软件和硬件的改进，人工智能目前发展得更快。即使目前最好的系统目前只有 1/100 的正确率，但 10 年后它可能几乎总是准确的。

我还想提醒人们，我写这篇文章的手机（iPhone 12）具有与 2003 年地球模拟器相同的计算能力。那是当时地球上最快的计算机。

想象一下这种发展并思考可能会发生什么变化。

People tend to forget how fast technological development advances. Even if you lived trough it you tend to forget how recently the world looked very different.

- before 2012 there was no smartphone

- before 2001 there was no wikipedia

- before 1995 less then 10 percent of the rich country home users had internet

- before 2023 there was no ai available to home users.

Hardware has been getting faster by a factor of 100 in 10 years and ~10‘000 in 20 years. Ai currently develops faster because of a combination of software and hardware improvements. Even if the best current system is only right 1/100 times right now, its likely nearly allways accurate in 10 years.

I also like to remind people that the phone i am writing this on (iphone 12), has the same computing power as the earth simulator in 2003. that was the fastest computer on the earth back then.

Imagine this development and think what changes might come.

jaen 2026/03/23 17:53
不完全是这样，2003 年的地球模拟器有 35.86 Linpack TFLOPS、10TB RAM 和 700TB 磁盘。

这仍然比 iPhone 12（0.02 Linpack TFLOPS、4GB RAM、256GB 存储）高出近三个数量级。

Not quite, the Earth Simulator in 2003 had 35.86 Linpack TFLOPS, 10TB of RAM and 700TB of disk.

That's still almost three orders of magnitude from the iPhone 12 (0.02 Linpack TFLOPS, 4GB RAM, 256GB storage).

在 HN 中查看
jaen 2026/03/23 22:00
这些 TFLOPS 数字毫无用处，因为它们是“营销峰值 TFLOPS”。在有意义的一般工作负载中，该计算能力与实际计算能力之间通常存在 10-100 倍的差异。

只有比较特定的、经过良好校准的基准测试才有意义，例如我所做的 Linpack。

Those TFLOPS numbers are quite useless as they are "marketing peak TFLOPS". There's usually a 10-100× difference between that and actual computational capabilities in meaningful general workloads.

It only makes sense to compare specific, well-calibrated benchmarks, such as Linpack, which is what I did.

wilkystyle 2026/03/23 21:27
技术进步很快，但末日叙事仍然是胡说八道。现实更接近于一直以来的情况：技术进步是增强人类能力的一种方式，而不是取代人类的能力。适应能力强并具有学习和成长的精神（一如既往）是成功职业生涯的关键因素。

通过足够狭窄的视角，任何技术进步都可以被视为威胁。如果你的工作是使用显微镜和计算器（计算机，职位名称）为公司进行计算，那么计算机（机器）的发明绝对对你的工作保障构成威胁。这并不是说不存在适应方面的挑战或如何做好适应的考虑，但旧方式一直是新方式的牺牲品。

我既不是反对人工智能，也不是人工智能传播者，但我认为更富有成效的观点是思考这些进步如何打开新机遇之门。例如，学习民主化。对于世界上任何拥有互联网连接和计算设备的人来说，获得私人数学导师或营养教练的帮助从未如此简单。

Technology advances quickly, but the apocalypse narrative is still bullshit. The reality is much closer to what it has always been: technological advances are a way to enhance what humans can do, not replace them. Being adaptable and adopting a spirit of learning and growth is (as it always has been) a key factor in a successful career trajectory.

Through a sufficiently narrow lens, any technological advancement can be perceived as a threat. If your job was to perform calculations for your company using a microscope and calculator (computer, the job title) then the invention of the computer (the machine) was absolutely a threat to your job security. That's not to say that there aren't challenges to adapting or considerations for how to do it well, but it has always been the case that the old way is a casualty of the new way.

I am neither anti-AI nor an AI evangelist but I think a more productive viewpoint is to think about how these advancements could open the door to new opportunity. For example, democratization of learning. It has never been easier for anyone in the world with an Internet connection and a computing device to have access to a personal math tutor or nutrition coach.

这将是一个重大变化，但只有那些将其视为生存线索的人——不再工作——才会面临真正的危险。我们的社会中有很多人不工作也过得很好。我们可以照顾我们的孩子，我们可以照顾我们的长辈。我们可以从事艺术并改善我们的世界，而不是竞争并比别人更好。

Why is it an apocalypse if people don't have jobs anymore? Why is it an apocalypse if the most intelligent being in the world isn't a human anymore.

It will be a major change, but only to people that see it as a thread to their existence - to not work anymore - are in real danger. There are tons of people in our society that are fine without working. We could tend to our children, we could tend to our elders. We could do arts and improve our world instead of competing and being better then others.

因为现在所有的迹象都指向新的锁定控制系统，而不是共同繁荣。这些公司被认为是非营利组织，他们被认为对改善事物进行了深入思考，但他们甚至无法从事物将如何改善中获得基本的叙述/哲学，而是转向盈利。

我们的权力系统被锁定在穴居人式的思想中，似乎无法创造新的东西，只是将新技术应用于非常非常古老、非常强制性的权力系统。技术乐观主义的日子已经一去不复返了，取而代之的是科技公司的狂热化，他们明确表示你会生活得更糟，这样他们就能获得更多利润，如果你无能为力阻止他们，他们就会迎合他们最糟糕的本能。

Because all signs right now are pointing to new locked in systems of control instead of shared prosperity. These companies were supposedly non-profits who were supposedly deep thinking on improving things but they can't even get a basic narrative/philosophy out of how things will improve and have instead pivoted to for profit.

Our systems of power are locked in caveman style thought and don't seem capable of creating something new, just applying new tech to very very old, very coercive systems of power. Gone are the techno optimist days replaced by the tech companies with enshitification with them explicitly stating you will live worse so that they can have more profit, and that if there is nothing you can do to stop them, they will cater to their worst instincts.

在 HN 中查看
danaris 2026/03/24 02:42
但我们也打了 严肃的 收益递减效应——对于硬件来说是过去十年，对于法学硕士来说是过去 2-3 年。

仅仅因为事情在 X 时间内取得了突飞猛进的发展，并不意味着它们将无限期地以相同（或增加）的速度继续这样做。

问题是，未来几年最终将取得巨大进展的是 通常非常不可预测，特别是如果我们关注过去几年的表面趋势。

鉴于我们对他们有更好的了解，而不是“哇，看看他们自 2018 年以来已经走了多远”，我们可以看到，法学硕士似乎不太可能在未来几年内再进步 100 倍。坦率地说，另外 100% 似乎不太可能。 （当然，量化 LLM 表现一开始就是一个棘手的命题……它在很大程度上是一个 定性的 努力。）

But we also hit serious diminishing returns effects—in the last decade for hardware, in the last 2-3 years for LLMs.

Just because things have advanced in leaps and bounds for X amount of time does not mean they will continue to do so at the same (or increasing) rates indefinitely.

The problem is that just what will end up being the thing that advances dramatically in the next few years is often very unpredictable, especially if one is looking at surface-level trends over the last few years.

Given what we, who have a better understanding of them than "wow, look how far they've come since 2018", can see, LLMs seem unlikely to advance by another 100x over the next several years. Frankly, another 100% seems unlikely. (Though of course, quantifying LLM performance is a tricky proposition to begin with...it is, to a large extent, a qualitative endeavor.)

intellectronica 2026/03/23 17:15
每天早上，火鸡都很高兴，对自己说：“哎呀，我真是一只幸运的火鸡，我什么都不用做，食物很丰富，我整天就吃着，吹着风，真是太棒了！”直到感恩节前一天的一个早晨，火鸡为即将迎来的美好一天而欢欣鼓舞……五分钟后却被人抱起来，拖到屠宰场。
Every morning the turkey rejoiced and said to himself "oh joy, I'm such a lucky turkey, I don't have to do anything, the food is plentiful, I just eat and shoot the breeze the whole day long, what an awesome life!" Until one morning, the day before thanksgiving, the turkey rejoiced about the awesome day he's about to have ... just to be picked up 5 minutes later and dragged to the slaughterhouse.

It’s a compelling story; but what you’re describing is, to the turkey, a black swan event, rather than an obvious inevitability that all the other turkeys keep telling the turkey is going to happen.

guzfip 2026/03/23 20:27
不，你得不到享乐主义的生活。火鸡过着享乐主义的生活，因为它的价值在于它的消费。对于它的主人来说，尽可能养肥它更有价值。

另一方面，你生来就不是为了被消费而生的。事实上，你越胖，你的成本就越高，用处也就越小。

因此，如果你没有被淘汰以获得免费资源，你所得到的更有可能是饥饿。

No you don’t get the hedonistic life. The turkey gets a hedonistic life because its value is in its consumption. It’s worth more to its owner fattened up as much as possible.

You on the other hand, are not bred to be consumed. And in fact the fatter you are, the more expensive and less useful you become.

So what you get is more likely starvation, if you aren’t culled to free resources.

在 HN 中查看
aurareturn 2026/03/23 17:25
假设您是一家中型企业。您一直希望提供最优质的客户服务，但以前无法做到，因为您需要雇用 5 个人才能做到这一点。相反，您从战略上决定不提供优质的客户服务，并以低于竞争对手的价格销售产品。所以你公司里没有客户服务人员。服务很差。它限制了增长。但为了在业务的其他地方获得优势，不提供良好的服务是战略性的。

但现在，您可以聘请 1 名客户服务人员，然后他们可以使用人工智能代理来提供最优质的客户服务。以前，您需要雇用 5 个人，这是不值得的。

所以你从没有客户服务员工变成了 1 名。

我怀疑这就是将会发生的事情。许多公司会雇用第一位或更多客户服务人员。许多大公司将解雇大部分客户服务人员。净效应实际上可能会增加客户服务就业总数。

我怀疑客户服务员工的职位空缺实际上会比现在更高，但公司将无法找到足够的人工智能技术人员来填补这一职位。我们将了解到职位空缺比以往任何时候都多，但公司却找不到他们所需的人工智能技能。这就是为什么我认为现在采用人工智能、学习它、理解它、擅长它的人将会有很高的需求。

Let's suppose you are a medium sized business. You've always wanted to provide top quality customer service but couldn't do it before because you'd need to hire 5 people to do it right. Instead, you strategically decided to not provide quality customer service and sell the product at a lower price than competitors. So you have no customer service person in the company. Service is bad. It limits growth. But it was strategic to not provide good service in order to gain an advantage somewhere else in the business.

But now, you can hire 1 customer service person, who could then use AI agents to provide the top quality customer service. Previously, you needed to hire 5 people, which wasn't worth it.

So you went from no customer service employee to 1.

I suspect that this is what will happen. Many companies will hire their first customer service person or more. Many big companies will layoff most of their customer service people. The net effect might actually increase total customer service employment.

I suspect that job openings for customer service employees will actually be higher than now but companies won't be able to find enough AI-skilled people to fill the job. We're going to read about how there are more job openings than ever but companies can't find the AI skillset they need. This is why I think people who adopt AI now, learn it, understand it, get good at it, will be in high demand.

在 HN 中查看
fastforwardius 2026/03/24 02:19
或者更有可能 - 您是一家大企业。

您提供客户支持，但认为这只是一个成本中心，因此您高兴地减少了 90% 的劳动力，并依靠人工智能来承担工作量。

这些非同寻常的案件最终由剩余的劳动力处理。他们精疲力竭，但幸运的是，有很多人在寻找工作。

Or more likely - you are a large business.

You provide customer support but think it's just a cost center so you happily reduce workforce by 90% and count on AI to cover the workload.

The non-trivial cases end up with the remaining workforce. They get burned out, but luckily there is plenty of people looking for any job.

在 HN 中查看
gloxkiqcza 2026/03/23 17:58
从某些方面来说，人工智能听起来几乎是乌托邦。我认为它可以在小型和大型企业之间更均匀地重新分配人力，使它们能够更公平地竞争并提高资本主义的效率（理想主义模型，而不是现实世界国家）。然而，你可能还记得，人工智能技术目前几乎完全由大型科技公司（及其下一代）控制，你必须问他们是否能够破坏这种改进，因为他们肯定会做最坏的事情，因为解放市场对他们没有好处。我们希望，尽管存在种种困难和当前的趋势，我们实际上已经达到了人工智能可以在本地/本地运行的状态，并且仍然有至少像今天一样开放的 SOTA 模型。
In some ways AI sounds almost utopian. I theory it could redistribute manpower more evenly between small and large businesses, allowing them to compete more fairly and improving the efficiency of capitalism (the idealistic model, not the real world state). However, than you remember that the AI tech is currently almost fully in control by the big tech (and its next generation) and you have to ask whether they’ll be able to sabotage that improvement because they will do their worst for sure since liberating the market is not beneficial to them. Let’s hope that despite all odds and current trends we actually reach a state where AI is possible to run on-prem/locally and there are still SOTA models at least as open as they are today.

ForHackernews 2026/03/23 18:47
对此强烈反对。计算贬值非常快。推理比训练更便宜。 DeepSeek 是对他们的警告，但大型人工智能公司不能在不危及他们“Wile E. Coyote off悬崖”经济的情况下改变方向。

LLM 表现已经趋于稳定；模型将变得更加高效。足够好的模型将部署在芯片上，就像 H.264 是一种足够好的视频编解码器但广泛使用一样。

Strongly dispute this. Compute very depreciates rapidly. Inference is cheaper than training. DeepSeek was the warning shot across their bow, but the big AI firms can't afford to change course without jeopardizing their "Wile E. Coyote off the cliff" economics.

LLM performance is already plateauing; models will get more efficient. Good-enough models will be deployed on chips, the same way H.264 is a good-enough video codec but used ubiquitously.

karmakurtisaani 2026/03/23 20:27
除了你的观点之外，我很好奇这些人工智能公司将如何在不使人工智能的使用变得异常昂贵的情况下实现盈利。前段时间，每次提示都是高补贴，我怀疑情况有多大变化。

编辑：也许你提到的模型效率是关键，我们拭目以待。

More than your points, I'm very curious how these AI companies are going to turn profit without making using AI insanely expensive. Some time ago, each prompt was highly subsidized, I doubt the picture has changed much.

Edit: maybe the model efficiency you mentioned is the key, we'll see.

在 HN 中查看
ForHackernews 2026/03/23 21:00
我怀疑他们不会。对于许多市场来说，先发劣势确实存在。每个人都知道亚马逊，但有多少人记得 Kozmo.com？

我的假设是 OpenAI、Anthropic 等将破产并最终被纳入 Microsoft/Google/ByteDance 等。新进入者将利用他们的开创性工作，以低廉的价格出售推理，而不需要投入大量的研发支出。

I suspect they just won't. First-mover disadvantage is real for many markets. Everyone knows Amazon, but how many remember Kozmo.com?

My assumption is that OpenAI, Anthropic, etc will go bankrupt and eventually be subsumed into Microsoft/Google/ByteDance & friends. New entrants will take their pioneering work and sell inference for pennies on the dollars without investing in massive R&D spend.

在 HN 中查看
pydry 2026/03/23 17:44
在我合作过的大量使用呼叫中心的公司中，他们通常分为三类：

* 客户想要人性化的接触

* 公司的系统被破坏了，如果客户能够快速、轻松地在网上做他们想做的事情，他们根本就不会打电话。

* 客户通常会感到愤怒，想要抱怨和/或理解，而公司却想拒绝他们。

人工智能对前两个没有帮助，它只帮助偏转（他们称之为最后一个）。

Of the companies that I've worked with that made significant use of call centres they generally fall into three groups:

* Customer wants the human touch

* The company's systems were broken and the customer wouldnt have called at all if they could quickly and easily do what they wanted online.

* Customers are routinely furious and want to complain and/or understand and the company wants to brush them off.

AI doesnt help the first two, it only helps with deflection (what they call the last one).

在 HN 中查看
truetraveller 2026/03/23 17:27
“顶级客户服务”和人工智能不能混为一谈。人们更讨厌人工智能的反应，而不是迟到的真实反应。
"Top customer service" and AI do not mix. People hate an AI response more than a late, real response.
在 HN 中查看
aurareturn 2026/03/23 17:33
不要迷失在客户服务示例中。专注于可以应用于许多其他职业的想法。

人们认为人工智能在阅读医学图像方面比人类更好，会让放射科医生失业。但相反，放射科医生的需求比以往任何时候都多，因为这使得扫描变得更便宜、更准确，从而带来了更多的客户需求。

客户服务也会发生同样的情况。人工智能使客户服务变得更便宜、更好、更快。更多的公司提供良好的客户服务以保持竞争力。越来越多的客户需要客户服务，因为现在更好了，他们期望如此，因为所有大小公司都可以负担得起优质的客户服务。

Don't get lost in the customer service example. Focus on the idea instead which can be applied to many other professions.

People thought AI being better than a human at reading medical images would put radiologists out of a job. But instead, radiologists had more demand than ever because it made getting a scan more affordable, more accurate which led to more customer demand.

Same can happen for customer service. AI makes customer service cheaper, better, faster. More companies offer good customer service in order to stay competitive. More customers demand customer service because it's better now and they expect it since all companies big or small can afford quality customer service.

在 HN 中查看
mikkupikku 2026/03/23 17:53
人工智能不需要做出回应，它可以用来将无意义的噪音从可操作的投诉中分类出来，而以前所有这些都会被忽略。 仅提出重要且可以解决的问题。
AI needn't respond, it can instead be used to sort the meaningless noise from the actionable complaints, where previously all would have been ignored. Raise only the issues that matter and can be addressed to the human.

truetraveller 2026/03/23 19:04

This I can somewhat agree to. But how much time is saved vs just skimming a support request by an agent? Or, just having filters for "keywords" like "sales" / "purchase" to increase priority?

在 HN 中查看
chinathrow 2026/03/23 17:35
您当然可以让代理尝试执行任务并在向客户发送回复之前进行监督。与此类似，我仍然检查代理生成的代码。

如果你把人从客户服务的循环中剔除，你将一无所获。

You sure could let the agent try to do the task and supervise it before sending of replies to customers. Similar to as to that I still check the code produced by an agent.

If you remove the human from the loop in customer service, you won't gain a thing.

在 HN 中查看
senordevnyc 2026/03/23 21:56
我经营着一家小型 SaaS 企业，该企业既向小型企业的非技术受众销售超级利基人工智能产品，又由人工智能构建和运营，并由我管理。我是这群人中唯一的人类。

我自己负责所有的销售和客户服务，因为这对我的客户来说是一个真正的卖点，如果他们有问题，他们可以与业主交谈，而且因为这些客户是我公司的命脉，我想尽可能接近他们和他们的需求。

但这仍然很耗时。

在客户服务方面，我对自动化的下一个突破将是让代理对入站请求进行分类，将需要采取的响应操作排队（取消帐户、升级、拆分团队等），然后将整个事情交给我批准并回复客户。仅此一点就可以轻松地将我花在 CS 上的时间减少 80%，同时保持更个性化的风格。我还应该指出，通过提供更多的自助服务选项、更好的文档等，可以减轻一些客户支持负担。但考虑到我的客户是非技术人员，总有一些人只是想快速发短信或电子邮件，因为他们讨厌技术，不想为此烦恼。

在销售方面，到目前为止，我一直是 100% 销售驱动的，但我想引入一种自助注册流程，该流程针对 80% 需求较简单且可能可以自行注册的客户，并为更大或更复杂的交易节省销售电话。

I run a small SaaS business that both sells a super niche AI product to a non-technical audience of small businesses, and also is built and run by AIs, managed by me. I'm the only human in the mix.

I do all the sales and customer service myself, because it's a genuine selling point for my customers that they can talk to the owner if they have issues, and because these customers are the lifeblood of my company, and I want to stay as close to them and their needs as I can.

But it's still time-consuming.

On the customer service side, my next crack at automation will just be having an agent triage inbound requests, queuing up the actions that need to be taken in response (cancel account, upgrade, split team, whatever), and then giving me the whole thing for approval and replying to customer. That alone should easily cut my time spent on CS by 80%, while keeping a more personal touch. I should also note that some of the customer support burden will be lifted by having more self-serve options to do things, better docs, etc. But given that my customers are non-technical, there are always going to be some of them that just want to dash off a text or email because they hate tech and don't want to hassle with it.

On the sales side, I've thus far been 100% sales driven, but I'd like to introduce a self-serve signup flow that targets the 80% of customers who have simpler needs and could probably sign up on their own, and save the sales calls for bigger or more complicated deals.

n4r9 2026/03/23 17:44
在最近的一次人工智能研讨会上，管理层明确表示，他们认为人工智能使冲刺和 Scrum 变得过时，看板更有意义，并且估计工作量/故事点也变得毫无意义。如果你问我的话，这是一个强烈的一线希望。
At a recent AI workshop management made clear that they see AI as rendering sprints and scrums obsolete, that Kanban makes a lot more sense, and that estimating effort/story-points is also becoming meaningless. Which is a strong silver lining if you ask me.
在 HN 中查看
odyssey7 2026/03/23 17:57
我想了解人工智能如何导致这个结果。
I want to understand how AI leads to this outcome.
在 HN 中查看
n4r9 2026/03/23 18:22
我认为这与瓶颈从代码生成转向指定、审查和集成代码有关。与人工智能代理合作生成规范、技术规范、代码和评论的过程更适合基于流程的结构（如看板）。

请记住，这是一家既有传统业务又有新建业务的 B2B 企业公司。管理层投入了大量资金来设计一个强大的、基于规范/上下文的工作流程来使用代理。其他地方可能会有所不同。

就我个人而言，即使在人工智能出现之前，我也不认为 scrum、计划、回顾等比看板更好，至少如果你的团队中有积极主动、聪明的人的话。它们实际上使事情变得不那么敏捷，并且故事点给人一种可预测性的错误感觉。在我看来，关键因素可能是人工智能代理很聪明并且处于开启状态（在正确的背景下）。

I think it's to do with the bottleneck shifting away from code generation and towards specifying and reviewing and integrating code. The process of working with AI agents to produce specs, tech specs, code, and reviews lends itself more to a flow-based structure (like kanban).

Bear in mind this is a B2B enterprise company with a mix of legacy and greenfield. And management has invested heavily into designing a robust spec/context-based workflow for using agents. Might be different elsewhere.

Personally I don't think scrums, planning, retros etc were better than kanban even before AI, at least if you have switched-on, motivated and smart people on your team. They actually made things less agile, and story-points give a false sense of predictability. Imo the crucial factor may be that AI agents are smart and switched-on (with the right context).

在 HN 中查看
Stromgren 2026/03/23 17:46
我参与构建了一个系统，该系统从特定行业的特殊形式的合同中读取结构化数据。价格、条款、提货、交付等。每个合同有几百个数据点。我们围绕如何展示和销售不完美的系统进行了多次讨论。问题是，现在的潜在客户正在手动抄写合同，我们很快意识到人们在这样做时犯了很多错误。当我们自己处理断言数据集时，这一点变得很明显。这不是一个完美的系统，您必须考虑如何使用数据（例如聚合价格索引），但我们实际上比人们每天必须转录数小时数据时所取得的成就更好。
I’ve been involved in building a system that reads structured data from a special form of contracts from a specific industry. Prices, clauses, pick up, delivery, etc. A couple hundred datapoints per contract. We had many discussions around how to present and sell an imperfect system. The thing is, the potential customers are today transcribing the contracts manually and we quickly realized that people make a ton of mistakes doing that. It became obvious when we were working on assertion datasets ourself. It’s not a perfect system and you have to consider how you use the data (aggregating for price indexing for instance), but we’re actually doing better than what people are achieving when they have to transcribe data for hours a day.

fabian2k 2026/03/23 17:34
我认为这里的论点有点稻草人，尽管其中也有一个很好的观点。人工智能不会实现所有客户支持的自动化，但它有潜力实现其中很大一部分的自动化。

其中的轶事是关于复杂的 B2B 企业软件。这并不是大部分客户支持，并且非常注重升级到真正的专家。

你不必取消 100% 的工作岗位才能​​产生巨大的影响。少数行业的大部分实现自动化已经会造成严重的破坏。

I think the argument here is a bit of a strawman, though there is a good point in there as well. AI will not automate all customer support, but it has the potential to automate a large fraction of it.

The anecdote in there is about complex B2B enterprise software. That's not the majority of customer support, and is very heavy on escalating to actual experts.

You don't have to remove 100% of the jobs to have huge effects. Automating large parts of a few sectors would already create significant disruptions.

windward 2026/03/23 17:58
>而且我觉得他们的计划更少

我认为这种心态必定有其迫在眉睫的末日。潜在生产力大幅提高，我们是否决定采取同样的做法，但成本更低？谁将资本分配给这种毫无生气的商品化？这一切感觉就像使用印刷机每月印刷一本圣经。

必须有一个可以提高生产力的角色。我们的技能不一定适合这些角色——而且角色可能更加分层——但有人将能够做得更多，得到更多的报酬。

>And I feel like they plan for less

I think this mentality must have its own imminent apocalypse. Gifted with an enormous increase in potential productivity, the decision is to do the same but cheaper? Who allocates capital to such spiritless commodification? It all feels like using a printing press to make one bible a month.

There must be a role that can be more productive. It might not necessarily be our skillsets that fit those roles - and the roles might be more stratified - but someone is going to be able to be do more, be paid more.

在 HN 中查看
skywhopper 2026/03/23 17:42
这篇文章确实谈到了这一点。容易自动化的东西并不能节省那么多钱。支持的巨额成本是您无法自动化的困难事情。
The article literally addresses this point. The easily automated stuff doesn’t save that much money. The big costs of support are the hard things you can’t automate.

在 HN 中查看
lelanthran 2026/03/23 17:46
老实说，无论如何，大部分工作很快就会进行重组。

“在将任务发送给任何人之前由法学硕士进行分类”几乎适用于任何事情，而不仅仅是支持电话。在另一个故事中，我看到有人提到他们想要一个类似广告拦截器的东西，但为了内容 - 一个“内容拦截器”。即使运行本地模型也不太难，通过浏览器扩展扫描当前页面并将其放入几个容器之一：逐字阅读、使用 ChatAI 进行总结、完全忽略、阅读并标记以供重新阅读。

软件开发？将票分为“复杂”、“简单”、“与首席开发人员交谈”。

软件提案？将提案分为“CotS 可用”、“FOSS 可用”、“快速开发”、“成本太高，无法进行”。

簿记？会计？他们都有可以分类的任务。

我听到你问，这一切意味着什么？好吧，如果某些垃圾箱是“ChatAI 和/或代理可以通过人工审核完成此操作”，那么您不再需要那么多员工。

所以，是的，一个 很多 如果这像他们所说的那样有效，那么很多人将会失业。

To be perfectly honest, the majority of work is going to see a restructure soon anyway.

"Triaging by LLM before sending task to any human" can work for almost anything, not just support calls. On another story I saw someone mention that they'd like something like an ad-blocker, but for content - a "content-blocker". Not too hard running even a local model that, via a browser extension, scnas the current page and places it into one of several bins: Read verbatim, summarise with ChatAI, Ignore completely, Read and mark for re-reading.

Software dev? Bin a ticket into "complex", "simple", "talk to lead dev".

Software proposal? Bin the proposal into "CotS available", "FOSS available", "Quick dev", "Too costly to proceed".

Bookkeeping? Accounting? They all have tasks that can be binned.

What does this all mean, I hear you ask? Well, you no longer need as many employees if some of the bins are "ChatAI and/or agent can complete this" with human review.

So, yeah, a lot of people are going to be out of work if this works like they say it does.

mjd 2026/03/23 17:52
或者这可能意味着他们生产出更有价值的产品，并且数量更多，因此需要更多的开发人员来完成。

如果开发人员为公司创造价值，然后公司可以自动化处理开发人员工作中最不有价值的部分，那么开发人员现在就更有价值。为什么公司会在这个时候解雇他们？

嗯，有些会，因为有些公司经营不善。其他人会利用这个机会。

Or it might mean they produce more valuable product and more of it and therefore need more devs to do it.

If a dev produces value for the company, and then the company can automate away the least valuable part of the dev's job, the dev is now more valuable. Why would tbe company get rid of them just at that moment?

Well, some will, because some companies are badly-run. Others will take advantage of the opportunity.

在 HN 中查看
lelanthran 2026/03/23 18:21
> 或者这可能意味着他们生产出更有价值的产品，而且数量更多，因此需要更多的开发人员来完成。

你假设公司生产的任何产品都有无限的需求。如果对其产品的需求是有限的，那么让 1 名开发人员生产 5 名开发人员的产出意味着该公司将让开发人员在一天的大部分时间里无所事事。

> 如果开发人员为公司创造价值，然后公司可以自动化处理开发人员工作中最不有价值的部分，那么开发人员现在就更有价值。

我不同意这个论点——一家公司需要多少发展是有一个实际限制的。过去，他们可能需要一个 10 人的团队才能满足这一限制。如果 2 人团队满足该限制，公司......具体会做什么？

毕竟，极限就是极限。

> Or it might mean they produce more valuable product and more of it and therefore need more devs to do it.

You're assuming unbounded demand for whatever product the company is producing. If demand for their product is bounded, having 1 dev produce the output of 5 devs means that the company is going to have devs simply sitting around doing nothing for most of the day.

> If a dev produces value for the company, and then the company can automate away the least valuable part of the dev's job, the dev is now more valuable.

I don't follow this argument - there is a practical limit to how much development a company requires. In the past they may have had a team of 10 to satisfy that limit. If the limit is satisfied by a team of 2 the company... does what exactly?

After all, a limit is a limit.

在 HN 中查看
mjd 2026/03/23 18:40
我工作过的每家公司都希望生产出更多更好的产品来卖更多的钱。有些人不能，因为他们的资源有限。

这些只想永远销售相同数量相同商品的企业在哪里？

Every company I have ever worked for has wanted to produce more better stuff to sell for more money. Some couldn't because they were resource constrained.

Where are these businesses that only ever want to sell the same amount of the same stuff forever?

在 HN 中查看
lelanthran 2026/03/23 18:52
> 我工作过的每家公司都希望生产出更多更好的产品来卖更多的钱。

我曾经加入过的每家公司也是如此，但与此同时，问题是 绝不 生产，始终是销售。

我所在的公司没有一个出现过“需求量很大，产量翻倍也满足不了”的问题。

这两件事同时成立——公司想要生产更多，但他们的生产率不是限制因素，销售率才是。

> 这些只想永远销售相同数量相同商品的企业在哪里？

我在哪里提出了这个主张？什么公司 想 是为了卖更多的东西，但生产并不是阻止他们卖更多东西的原因。

公司产量增加一倍并不会导致销售额增加一倍 - 一个公司的产量增加 绝不 导致另一个。

> Every company I have ever worked for has wanted to produce more better stuff to sell for more money.

So has every company I've ever been in, but at the same time, there problem was never production, it was always sales.

No company I have ever been in had the problem of "demand is so large that even if we double output we still cannot satisfy it".

Both things are true at the same time - companies want to produce more, but their rate of production is not the limiting factor, the rate of sales is.

> Where are these businesses that only ever want to sell the same amount of the same stuff forever?

Where did I make that claim? What companies want is to sell more stuff, but production is not what is preventing them from selling more stuff.

Doubling production in a company does not lead to doubling sales - an increase in one never causes the other.

在 HN 中查看
mjd 2026/03/23 21:48
在我工作过的公司中，销售人员经常对管理层说这样的话：

* 我无法销售我们的产品，因为我们竞争对手的产品具有某种功能。 我们多久可以拥有该功能？ * 我无法进行任何新的销售，但潜在客户不断告诉我他们需要解决类似问题。 我们可以扩大我们的产品线吗？ * 有些客户可能正在使用我们产品的某些功能，但他们发现它太混乱了。 对此我们能做些什么呢？ * 一位大客户告诉我，他们有一个问题我们当前的产品无法解决，所以我告诉他们我们可以在下个月初解决这个问题

正如您所说，销售部门是开发工作的驱动者，而不是相反。

In companies I've worked for, Sales has often come to management saying things like:

* I couldn't sell our product because our competitor's has a certain feature. How soon can we have that feature? * I can't make any new sales, but prospective customers keep telling me they need a solution for a similar problem. Could we expanded our product line? * Some customers could be using a certain feature of our product, but they find it too confusing. What could we do about this? * A big customer told me they have a problem our current product doesn't solve, so I told them we would be able to solve it by the beginning of next month

As you say, the sales department is the driver of development work, not vice versa.

在 HN 中查看
lelanthran 2026/03/23 22:41
我目前在 在 销售渠道，以及 全部 客户说了这样的话，然后在报价发出后就放弃了。它是如此常见，现在甚至很容易发现：

1. 当某个客户的经理说 “如果您在产品中添加 $FOO，我们需要花多少钱？” 我什至懒得用我发送给他们的报价来更新销售预测。

2. 当他们说“我们多久可以得到这个？”时， 那是 当我实际更新销售预测时。

因此，如果您的销售人员说“看，顾客说只要我们有 $FOO，他们就会和我们一起去”，那么他们就没有通过妈妈测试[1] - 他们说话的人只是不想太消极，不知道如何对一个迷人且讨人喜欢的人说“不”[2]，等等。

销量是市场需求的函数。例如，当需求为 200 单位/米时，产量加倍并不能让你卖出 400 单位/米。

另外，听起来你的论点仅针对软件产品，这是一个 微小的 经济的一部分。我真正谈论的是销售非软件产品/服务的公司 - 他们的销售额是 不是 受到软件开发的限制，也受到其市场范围的限制。

即使这些公司的开发人员人数增加一倍，对收入的影响也几乎为零。

我的意思是，看，我可以看到你在这里真诚地争论，所以我正在尝试做同样的事情，但 IME 生产力根本对收入没有任何影响，它所能做的就是降低成本。

----------------

[1] 这是一本简短而有价值的读物，我向所有我遇到的试图从事销售工作的人推荐它。

[2] 如果你没有魅力或不讨人喜欢，那么你一开始就不应该从事销售工作。

I'm currently in the sales channel, and all customers say things like this, then back off the minute a quote is sent through. It's so common it's even easy to spot now:

1. When a manager at some client says "How much will it costs us for you to add $FOO to the product?" I don't even bother updating the sales forecast with the quote I send them.

2. When they say "How soon can we have this?", that's when I actually update the sales forecasts.

So if your sales guys are saying "Look, customer said they'd go with us if only we had $FOO", they're failing the Mom Test[1] - the person they spoke just didn't want to be too negative, didn't know how to say "No" to a charming and likeable person[2], etc.

Sales is a function of the demand in the market. When the demand is (for example) 200 units/m of something, doubling your output does not let you sell 400 units/m.

Also, it sounds that your argument is for software products only, which is a tiny part of the economy. I was really talking about companies that sell non-software products/services - their sales is not limited by software development, it's limited by their market reach.

Even if those companies doubled their developer headcount, it'll have pretty much zero impact on revenue.

I mean, look, I can see you're arguing in good faith here, so I'm trying to do the same, but IME productivity simply doesn't have any effect on revenue, all it can do is lower costs.

----------------

[1] This is such a short and valuable read, that I recommend it to everyone I meet who is trying to do sales.

[2] If you're not charming or likeable, then you shouldn't be in sales in the first place.

在 HN 中查看
jeremie_strand 2026/03/24 05:51
真正的问题不是白领工作是否会在一夜之间消失，而是技能底线的上升速度是否会比大多数工人能够适应的速度更快。从历史上看，新技术创造了相邻的角色——但时间尺度是几十年，而不是三五年。这是一个有意义的不同约束。
The real question isn't whether white-collar jobs disappear overnight, but whether the skills floor keeps rising faster than most workers can adapt. Historically, new technology created adjacent roles - but the timescale was decades, not 3-5 years. That's a meaningfully differnent constraint.

mikkupikku 2026/03/23 18:29

In between the brainless masses mindlessly regurgitating press releases (most of whom are human, not bots) or reddit doomerisms from the other side, there are tons of people discussing real successes and failures they've had with the tech. If you can't see the middle, it's probably because you're in one of the extremes.
在 HN 中查看
keiferski 2026/03/23 17:46
分叉是正确的模型，而且它已经发生了：

对于最终客户不关心他们是否与人工智能交互、通过人工智能阅读内容等的事情——或者如果公司不关心客户的想法（参见：过去二十年的自动电话客户支持热线）——这些工作将被人工智能工作取代。例子包括任何类型的死记硬背的文档、博客图像等通用数字资产创建、低水平的客户支持，以及公司并不真正关心客户的大多数事情，因为公司无论如何都会得到报酬。

如果它 做 无论最终客户怎么想，这个角色本质上都会变得越来越人性化。例如高端企业销售、个性和专业知识驱动的媒体和内容，以及任何被“揭露”为人工智能的东西都会被负面看待。

Bifurcation is the right model and it’s already happening:

For things where the end customer doesn’t care if they’re interacting with an AI, reading content by an AI, etc. – or if the company doesn’t care what the customer thinks (see: automated phone customer support lines for the last twenty years) – the work will be replaced by AI work. Examples are any kind of rote documentation, generic digital asset creation like blog images, low level customer support, and most things where the company doesn’t really care about the customer, because the company is getting paid regardless.

If it does matter what the end customer thinks, the role will become increasingly humanistic in nature. Examples are high-end enterprise sales, personality and expertise-driven media and content, and anything where being “revealed” as an AI is perceived negatively.

wait what? I am in india and jobs are being lost by thousands everyday in IT field.

infact, i go and implement dumb AI models in many companies and executives immediately show "how many people they can fire with this advancement".

在 HN 中查看
skywhopper 2026/03/23 17:47
全球 IT 服务中存在着令人难以置信的巨大浪费，特别是在咨询和离岸外包领域，该领域的大幅波动、上下波动实际上与什么有效或无效没有任何关系。离岸工作或放弃离岸合同的决定是基于最新的炒作周期，而不是它是否有效或值得。

因此，虽然可能有很多顾问失业，但这并不是因为人工智能工具可以更好地完成工作。这是因为管理层认为投资者会接受人工智能工具可以更好地完成工作并节省资金的故事。管理层和投资者不知道，无法判断，而且老实说，他们并不真正关心它是好是坏。而且他们的管理非常糟糕，无论如何都无法判断。

There is such a mind-bogglingly huge amount of waste in IT services worldwide, particularly in the consulting and offshoring areas, that big swings, up and down, in that area don’t actually have anything to do with what works well or doesn’t. Decisions are made to offshore work or drop offshore contracts based on the latest hype cycle, not whether it is effective or worthwhile.

So while there may be lots of consultants losing their jobs, that’s not because AI tools do the work better. It’s because management thinks investors will accept the story that AI tools will do the work better and save money. Management, and investors, don’t know, can’t judge, and honestly don’t actually care if it’s better or worse. And they run things so poorly it would be impossible to tell anyway.

tamimio 2026/03/23 17:53
当然是这样，这只是降低工资的替罪羊，是另一种权力动态对员工的伎俩。我注意到很多管理者选择合作+人工智能组合或外包+人工智能，认为这是最小化开支和最大化利润的终极金矿，但他们很快就遇到了现实检验。不幸的是，当他们这样做时，他们并没有解决根本原因问题，而是只雇用了团队中的一名高级人员，让他超负荷工作，同时赞扬人工智能如何提高了生产力等。
Of course it is, it’s just a scapegoat to lower the wages, another power dynamic trick pulled on employees. I have noticed a lot of managers going with coop+AI combo or outsourcing+AI, thinking it’s the ultimate goldmine to minimize expenses and maximize profits, and they soon hit a reality check. And when they do, unfortunately, instead of resolving the root cause issue, they go and hire only one senior in the team and overload and overwork him, while praising the AI how it increased the productivity and all.

在 HN 中查看
jonathanstrange 2026/03/23 17:42
我目前最大的担忧甚至与工作无关，而是企业和当局将使用人工智能来处理客户/客户关系，但这种人工智能不会被允许做出任何重大改变，因此完全是浪费时间。在许多地方，这可能会使本已严峻的局势变成一场绝对的噩梦。更糟糕的是，当局——可能还有企业——可能会禁止或阻止用户人工智能代理，因此你甚至无法使用自己的人工智能与他们的人工智能进行谈判。

这是立法者需要尽快解决的问题。需要有与人类交谈的权利，或者（也许是过于技术乐观的路线）禁止没有足够决策权的人工智能。

My biggest worry currently isn't even job-related, it's that corporations and authorities will use AI for customer/client relations but that this AI will not be allowed to make any significant changes and is therefore an utter waste of time. In many places, this could turn an already dire situation into an absolute nightmare. What might make it even worse is that authorities - and probably also corporations - will likely ban or block user AI agents, so you cannot even use your own AI to negotiate with their AI.

That's something that needs to be addressed by lawmakers ASAP. There needs to be a right to speak to a human, or (the perhaps overly tech optimistic route) a prohibition of AI that doesn't have adequate decision-making power.
